concepts.title: Abscissa of convergence
concepts.blurb: A Dirichlet series $\sum a_n n^{-s}$ converges in a right half-plane $\Re(s)>\sigma_a$. The boundary $\sigma_a$ captures growth of partial sums and controls where Euler-product manipulations are justified.

concepts.title: Euler product factorization
concepts.blurb: When coefficients are multiplicative, a Dirichlet series factors as an infinite product over primes. This analytic encoding of unique factorization is the bridge from arithmetic data to complex-analytic behavior.

concepts.title: Primes in arithmetic progressions
concepts.blurb: Dirichlet characters split residue classes, and nonvanishing of $L(1,\chi)$ for nontrivial $\chi$ forces infinitely many primes in each reduced class $a \bmod q$.
